source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the physical architecture of a student hybrid rocket motor (N2O/HTPB, 60 bar MEOP, 6 s burn). The original prompt explicitly forbids welds and brazed joints in the chamber body, which makes the chamber itself a single machined shell; however the full motor is an assembly of the chamber shell, the injector-side closure stack, the nozzle retainer stack, oxidizer feed fittings, the burst-disk port body, the fuel grain casing insert, and the igniter port. All speculation marked.

full_prompt:
Design a single-part, monolithic combustion chamber (case) for a student-researched hybrid rocket motor intended for 30 km (30,000 ft) apogee. Oxidizer is nitrous oxide (N2O); fuel grain is HTPB. The chamber is a seamless, spin-formed or machined monolithic cylinder with two integral bosses: a forward injector-bulkhead interface (8x M6 bolt pattern on a 120 mm PCD) and an aft nozzle-retention interface (12x M8 bolt pattern on a 140 mm PCD). Internal diameter 140 mm, internal length 700 mm, wall thickness to be sized. Material is 6061-T6 aluminum with derating for hot-fire temperature 150 C (ablative liner protects the wall): Young's modulus 65 GPa at 150 C, yield strength 240 MPa at 150 C, ultimate strength 276 MPa at 150 C, density 2700 kg/m^3. Maximum Expected Operating Pressure (MEOP) is 60 bar (6 MPa) chamber pressure with a transient pressure spike allowance of 1.1 x MEOP = 66 bar. Structural requirements: (R1) proof pressure at 1.5 x MEOP = 90 bar held for 2 minutes, max von Mises <= yield (hot) / 1.1 = 218 MPa; (R2) burst pressure 2.5 x MEOP = 150 bar with ultimate margin >= ultimate (hot) / 1.25 = 221 MPa max von Mises; (R3) the emergency depressurization burst disk port must be integral to the chamber wall and sized to vent chamber to <= 10% MEOP = 6 bar within 5 seconds following activation; (R4) dry mass of the chamber case <= 3.5 kg; (R5) external thermal boundary wall temperature must not exceed 150 C during 6.0 s nominal burn with an ablative liner of EPDM/silica-phenolic (this thermal constraint is the binding design driver, not pure structural). Verification: primary structural FEA with the hot-derated properties at proof and burst; secondary thermal analysis (transient 1D conduction through ablative liner + chamber wall) to confirm R5; vibration and buckling secondary if explicitly requested. Fluid (CFD) and radiation not required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242, materials certificate, and a report listing wall thickness in mm, dry mass in kg, max von Mises at proof and burst in MPa, emergency-vent time in s, peak external wall temperature in C during burn.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured from a single monolithic case to a motor-level multi-part assembly. The parts are: the machined chamber outer shell (still monolithic, still no welds/brazes in the pressure wall), the forward injector plate / closure bulkhead bolted via the 8x M6 pattern on a 120 mm PCD, the aft nozzle retainer/converging-diverging nozzle stack bolted via the 12x M8 pattern on a 140 mm PCD, the oxidizer N2O feed-line fitting welded to a dedicated dome boss, the emergency burst-disk port body that screws into an integral chamber-wall boss, the HTPB fuel-grain casing that nests inside the chamber bore, and the pyrotechnic igniter port plug. The pressure-containing shell remains inherently monolithic (the original prohibition on welds/brazes in the chamber wall is preserved). FEM verification runs on the assembled motor only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) at the two bolt patterns, the burst-disk boss, and the feed-line port must be <= 1.0 under proof (LC1) and burst (LC2). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part, monolithic combustion chamber (case) for a student-researched hybrid rocket motor intended for 30 km (30,000 ft) apogee. Oxidizer is nitrous oxide (N2O); fuel grain is HTPB.

The chamber is a seamless, spin-formed or machined monolithic cylinder with two integral bosses: a forward injector-bulkhead interface (8x M6 bolt pattern on a 120 mm PCD) and an aft nozzle-retention interface (12x M8 bolt pattern on a 140 mm PCD). Internal diameter 140 mm, internal length 700 mm.

Material is 6061-T6 aluminum with derating for hot-fire temperature 150 C: Young's modulus 65 GPa at 150 C, yield strength 240 MPa at 150 C, ultimate strength 276 MPa at 150 C, density 2700 kg/m^3. MEOP is 60 bar (6 MPa) chamber pressure with a transient pressure spike allowance of 1.1 x MEOP = 66 bar.

Load cases: LC1 proof pressure at 1.5 x MEOP = 90 bar held for 2 minutes; LC2 burst pressure 2.5 x MEOP = 150 bar; LC3 emergency depressurization vent chamber to <= 10% MEOP = 6 bar within 5 seconds; LC4 thermal burn 6.0 s nominal burn with external wall temperature not exceeding 150 C.

prompt.title: Student hybrid combustion chamber motor assembly (30 kft apogee class) (multi-part)
prompt.brief: Student hybrid rocket motor assembly at 60 bar MEOP, N2O/HTPB, 6 s burn. Assembled from a machined 6061-T6 chamber outer shell, an injector-plate bulkhead (8x M6 PCD 120), a nozzle-retainer stack (12x M8 PCD 140), an oxidizer feed-line fitting, an integral burst-disk port body, the fuel-grain casing insert, and an igniter port plug. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under proof and burst.

load_cases.name: emergency_vent
load_cases.description: vent from MEOP to <=10% MEOP within 5 s

boundary_conditions.pressure: uniform on chamber inner wall and all wetted part surfaces
boundary_conditions.thermal: convection + ablative liner on inner wall, free convection outside

parts.name: chamber_outer_shell
parts.role: primary pressure-containing cylindrical shell with integral forward and aft flanges and an integral burst-disk boss on the sidewall; machined or spin-formed from a single billet per the original monolithic-wall requirement

integral_features: forward flange with 8x M6 tapped/through on 120 mm PCD
aft flange with 12x M8 tapped/through on 140 mm PCD

parts.name: injector_plate_closure_bulkhead
parts.role: forward closure disc carrying the N2O injector orifice pattern and sealing against the chamber forward flange; transfers axial thrust load back into the chamber shell through the 8x M6 bolt pattern

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 8x M6 on 120 mm PCD (matches chamber forward flange)
geometric_constraints.center_feature: injector orifice array (team-selected count) and oxidizer-inlet port

parts.name: nozzle_retainer_stack
parts.role: converging-diverging nozzle block + aft retaining flange; bolts to the chamber aft flange and reacts the full chamber pressure x throat-area axial load; includes the graphite/ablative throat insert
parts.count_note: Functionally one aft subassembly; teams frequently split into (nozzle insert) + (retainer flange) = 2 bodies.

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 12x M8 on 140 mm PCD (matches chamber aft flange)

parts.material_hint: 6061-T6 aluminum retainer with graphite or phenolic throat insert

parts.name: oxidizer_feed_line_fitting
parts.role: N2O feed-line port body threaded into / sealed to the injector-plate oxidizer inlet; transmits oxidizer-line axial and bending reactions into the forward closure

geometric_constraints.orientation: perpendicular to injector plate, aligned with tank feed

parts.name: burst_disk_port_body
parts.role: emergency-vent port body that threads into the integral boss on the chamber sidewall and retains the burst disk; must vent chamber to <= 10% MEOP within 5 s (R3)

geometric_constraints.vent_bore_mm: sized to satisfy R3 discharge calculation at 6 MPa upstream
geometric_constraints.thread: team-selected, sized to seal at >= 150 bar burst margin

parts.material_hint: 316 stainless steel body with nickel burst disk

parts.name: fuel_grain_casing_insert
parts.role: HTPB fuel-grain carrier tube and EPDM/silica-phenolic ablative liner assembly that nests inside the chamber bore; does not carry pressure load but bounds the gas-side heat flux that drives R5
parts.material_hint: HTPB fuel grain + EPDM/silica-phenolic ablative sleeve

parts.name: igniter_port_plug
parts.role: threaded plug in an auxiliary injector-plate port for pyrotechnic igniter insertion and post-burn sealing

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Z-axis along chamber centerline, +Z = aft (nozzle exit direction)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (forward 8x M6 bolt pattern, aft 12x M8 bolt pattern, burst-disk boss threaded interface, feed-line port threaded interface) shall be <= 1.0 under the governing load cases LC1 (proof) and LC2 (burst).
No leakage at any sealed interface under LC1 proof hold (120 s at 90 bar); assembled-level pressure decay <= 0.5 bar over hold.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

analysis_types: linear static (proof, burst) on assembled model
transient thermal (6 s burn) on assembled model

interfaces.mediated_by: 8x M6 bolts on 120 mm PCD with static face seal
interfaces.load_path: forward axial pressure load (P * A_bore) from injector plate into chamber forward flange; bolt pattern carries tensile preload plus pressure-separation load

interfaces.mediated_by: 12x M8 bolts on 140 mm PCD with static face seal
interfaces.load_path: aft axial thrust reaction (P * A_throat + P * A_bore) from nozzle retainer into chamber aft flange; bolts carry tensile preload plus thrust-separation load
N2O supply line pressure and line bending into injector plate

interfaces.mediated_by: threaded boss with metal-to-metal or soft-seated seal
interfaces.load_path: chamber pressure into burst-disk body; under LC3 the disk ruptures and the port flows at choked mass-flux

interfaces.mediated_by: slip fit with forward bulkhead retention (grain is trapped between B and C)
interfaces.load_path: grain radial swell and gas-side heat flux into ablative liner into chamber inner wall
chamber pressure sealed after ignition event

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled motor; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. The proof (LC1) and burst (LC2) cases bound every sealed interface simultaneously, so assembled-level analysis is the correct granularity.

notes.why_multi_part: A working hybrid motor requires the chamber shell plus a forward closure, an aft nozzle retainer, an oxidizer feed fitting, a burst-disk body, a fuel-grain insert, and an igniter port. The original prompt's 'monolithic chamber' language applies only to the pressure-containing cylindrical wall, which is preserved as part A. Every other part is inherent to the motor's function.
notes.speculation_caveats: Part dimensions beyond those fixed by the original sample (internal_diameter, internal_length, two bolt patterns, burst-disk port location) are team-selected and marked speculation:true.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
